title: Ljiljana Stanic
content:
People.ContactEmail lstanic@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
Ljiljana cuts through legal complexity to find straightforward business results.
She represents companies in contentious, high-stakes disputes, with expertise in the mining sector and a particular focus on shareholder disputes and securities litigation, corporate governance matters, class actions, and procurement matters. Ljiljana has significant experience in fiduciary and breach of trust issues, and particular expertise in commercial and shareholder disputes involving trust and estate law issues (including high net worth family disputes and other matters involving family offices). Ljiljana also helps international and domestic clients navigate the dynamic landscape of international trade and sanctions regulations. She brings her strengths in advocacy and incisive, solutions-focused approach to bear in her trade law practice, which centres on enforcement and compliance matters relating to economic sanctions, tariffs, and trade and export controls, as well as investment treaty arbitration.
Clients value Ljiljana’s deep insight and pragmatic advice, as well as her strength as an advocate and commitment to client service. In interviews with Chambers and Partners , clients praise Ljiljana’s “ commercial vision ” and “ sophistication ”, and describe her as “ a brilliant and knowledgeable advocate ”, “ highly capable, intelligent, and personable ” and “ just excellent ”.
Ljiljana has acted as lead or co-counsel before the Supreme Court of Canada, the Federal Court of Appeal, the Federal Court of Canada, and all levels of court in Ontario. She has also represented clients before arbitral and administrative tribunals, including the Canadian International Trade Tribunal and the first binational panel under the Canada-United States-Mexico Agreement (CUSMA/USMCA/T-MEC).

#### Ljiljana’s recent significant private law mandates include:
- representation of a royalty holder in an investor state dispute
- successful representation of corporate consultants in a high-stakes governance dispute addressing fiduciary duties in the context of departing fiduciaries and the indoor management rule ( Métis National Council Secretariat Inc. v. Chartier , 2025 ONSC 6150 )
- successful representation of a private mining company in a fair value application
- successful representation of a public mining company in a private arbitration addressing minority shareholder oppression and dissent claims
- representation of financial institution in a case determining duties owed to former beneficiaries
- assisting clients with Canadian economic sanctions-related litigation and advisory matters, including securing required permits and authorizations
- resisting an attempt by defendant insurers to force individual plaintiffs into an unwieldly class proceeding
- successfully representing the winner of a procurement in five judicial review applications ( Heiltsuk Horizon Maritime Services Ltd. v. Atlantic Towing Limited , 2021 FCA 26 )
- representing a Canadian bank in connection with litigation under Title III of the Helms-Burton Act
- representing a foreign national in a judicial review application concerning the application of sanctions law
- successfully resolving complex disputes involving business structuring and estates and trusts issues
- conducting an investigation of the anti-corruption and compliance program of a major multinational corporation
- representing the Canadian Chamber of Commerce in TELUS Communications Inc. v. Wellman, an appeal to the Supreme Court of Canada concerning the enforcement of arbitration agreements in proposed class proceedings
- representing a foreign company in a multi-jurisdiction price-fixing class action
- representing an international company in a $400 million Canadian class action alleging unlawful competition
- successfully representing a multi-national defence contractor in defending a procurement contract
Ljiljana also maintains a leading public law, constitutional, and pro bono practice. Her significant mandates include leading cases regarding equality rights and constitutional remedies, including Egale Canada v Alberta ( 2025 ABKB 394 ), UR Pride Centre for Sexuality and Gender Diversity v Government of Saskatchewan ( 2025 SKCA 74 , 2024 SKKB 23 , 2023 SKKB 204 ), Ontario (Attorney General) v. G , 2020 SCC 38 . She also serves as lead supervising lawyer for the 519 Trans ID Clinic, which assists trans and gender diverse individuals in updating their identification. In recognition of her contributions to advancing equality for LGBTQ2+ communities, she has received the Canadian Bar Association Sexual and Gender Diversity Alliance (SAGDA) Ally Award and the Laleh Moshiri Ally Award. Ljiljana is committed to providing pro bono litigation and advisory assistance to individuals and organizations where it will have the greatest impact, and practising in a trauma-informed manner.
Ljiljana serves on the Securities Proceedings Advisory Committee of the Ontario Securities Commission and as the Chair of the CBA International Law section. She has previously served as Chair and Past Chair of the OBA International Law section, and has been an active member of the OBA’s Civil Litigation section executive. She is also a member of the Law Society of Ontario, the Canadian Bar Association, the Ontario Bar Association, and the Advocates’ Society.
Ljiljana serves as the Vice Chair of the Board of Directors of Theatre Passe Muraille, an alternative Canadian theatre company dedicated to supporting and showcasing marginalized voices and independent and emerging artists and companies.
Ljiljana received her law degree from the University of Toronto, where she was Co-Editor-in-Chief of the University of Toronto Faculty of Law Review and a member of the Grand Moot team. Ljiljana also studied at the University of Copenhagen Faculty of Law, with a focus on international and EU trade and development law. Prior to entering law, Ljiljana received a SSHRC grant to conduct her graduate studies in epistemology and metaphysics at the University of Toronto, from which she holds both Honours Bachelor’s and Master’s degrees in philosophy.
